arts.ttl: Transfer rumors, news: Man City eye Gibbs-White from Nottingham Forest
arts.sum: Manchester City are interested in signing Nottingham Forest's Morgan Gibbs-White. Transfer Talk has the latest news and gossip.

arts.ttl: Everton vs Man City: Preview, predictions and lineups
arts.sum: Previewing Everton's clash with Man City in the Premier League, including team news, predictions and how to watch on TV.

arts.ttl: Pep Guardiola confirms stance on 22-year-old transfer which could save Man City millions
arts.sum: Manchester City manager Pep Guardiola confirms that he wants one of his out-of-favour men to stay at the Etihad this summer.

arts.ttl: Guardiola fears for summer transfers if Manchester City miss out on top five
arts.sum: Manager believes targets may prefer to move elsewhere ‘I’m sure the players look at ones in Champions League’ Pep Guardiola fears Manchester City could miss out on summer transfer targets if they fail to qualify for the Champions League. After winning the last four Premier League titles during an unprecedented spell of domestic dominance, the goal this season is to earn a place back at European football’s top table. City sit fifth having recovered from an awful end to 2024, but they have work to do in the fight for a Champions League spot that may have a bearing on who they can attract this summer. Continue reading...

arts.ttl: Guardiola on squad selection for Everton trip
arts.sum: “I don’t like a big squad and now one or two players cannot even travel to Goodison Park,” he told the media in his pre-match press conference. Throughout the current season, City have been plagued by injuries to key players, a situation that prompted investment into the squad in January with Omar Marmoush, Abdukodir Khusanov and Vitor Reis all signing and Claudio Echeverri arriving too.

arts.ttl: Moyes believes Everton’s form has opened players’ eyes to their true potential
arts.sum: David Moyes’ side have collected 21 points since January ‘Some are standing up and doing really well,’ he says David Moyes believes Everton’s recent form against leading Premier League teams has opened players’ eyes to their true potential and influenced his decision on their futures. The Everton manager admits he did not expect many points from an April schedule of Liverpool, Arsenal, Nottingham Forest, Manchester City and Chelsea when he studied the fixture list on his return to the club. Everton’s opponents were the league’s top five at the start of the month. A narrow defeat at Liverpool, a draw with Arsenal and victory at Forest, however, have altered perceptions of Moyes and his players before they encounter Pep Guardiola’s side at Goodison Park on Saturday. Continue reading...

arts.ttl: Pep Guardiola makes startling Man City transfer admission amid Champions League race
arts.sum: Manchester City have Champions League qualification in their own hands - but face stiff competition from five other teams for just three places.

arts.ttl: Pep Guardiola admits Man City could miss out on top transfer targets this summer
arts.sum: Manchester City are still fighting to earn a place in next season's Champions League after a nightmare campaign leaves them battling for a top-four spot over the final weeks
